key_insight: An agentic multi-model pipeline can autoformalize research mathematics in Lean 4, including extending missing library concepts before proving the target theorem.
engineering_relevance: This shows a practical path for turning informal mathematical research into machine-checked artifacts, improving reliability for theorem proving and formal methods workflows.
method: Orchestrator coordinates multiple agents for translation, proof search, and validation.
Auxiliary Lemma technique validates new type definitions before formalizing main theorems.
Uses general coding LLMs rather than Lean-specialized small models.
evidence_signal: The system produced machine-checked Lean proofs for 32 PutnamBench problems and successfully formalized and proof-checked main theorems from five STOC papers, with human validation and two proofs requiring no axioms beyond Lean's kernel.
watch_item: Performance may depend on careful orchestration and on correctly extending the formal library, so errors in auxiliary definitions or assumptions could still propagate.
paper_title: Beyond the Library: An Agentic Framework for Autoformalizing Research Mathematics
